lessons.title: Hardware and Software
objectives: Identify internal and external hardware components
Differentiate between system and application software
lessons.experiment: Virtual PC Build: Drag and drop internal components like CPU and RAM onto a virtual motherboard and connect peripherals.

lessons.title: Data Representation
objectives: Convert between binary, decimal, and hexadecimal
Understand bits, bytes, and storage capacities
concepts: Units of Storage (KB, MB, GB, TB)
lessons.experiment: Binary Converter: Interactive tool to toggle bits on/off and see the resulting decimal and character representations.

lessons.title: Problem Solving & Algorithms
objectives: Develop algorithms using pseudocode and flowcharts
lessons.experiment: Flowchart Builder: Assemble a flowchart for a logical process (e.g., 'Grade Calculator') using drag-and-drop symbols.

lessons.title: Programming Constructs
objectives: Implement code using variables, constants, and loops
lessons.experiment: Code Playground: Write and run simple pseudocode/Pascal-like code in a live editor to solve mathematical problems.

lessons.title: Database Design
objectives: Define tables, fields, records, and primary keys
lessons.experiment: Schema Designer: Create a database schema for a school system, defining tables and linking them with primary keys.

lessons.title: Queries and Reports
objectives: Extract data using single and multi-criteria queries
lessons.experiment: Database Query Builder: Drag fields and criteria to build SQL queries visually and see the resulting data set.

lessons.title: Network Types & Topologies
objectives: Differentiate between LAN, WAN, and MAN
lessons.experiment: Network Topology Drag: Connect devices into star, bus, or ring topologies and test the network connectivity.

lessons.title: Internet Security & Ethics
objectives: Identify security threats and protection methods
lessons.experiment: Security Auditor: Review a series of simulated network configurations and emails to identify and fix security vulnerabilities.

lessons.title: HTML Fundamentals
objectives: Create basic web pages using HTML tags
concepts: Tags (Head, Body, H1, P, A, IMG)
lessons.experiment: Live HTML Editor: Edit HTML code on one side and see the rendered webpage update in real-time.

lessons.title: Styling & Interactivity
objectives: Apply basic styling with CSS and logic with JavaScript
lessons.experiment: Style-a-Page: Use a CSS panel to change colors, fonts, and layouts of a template webpage instantly.

lessons.title: Advanced Word Processing
objectives: Use mail merge and advanced formatting
lessons.experiment: Mail Merge Simulator: Link a data source to a template letter and generate personalized invites for a group.

lessons.title: Spreadsheet Formulas & Charts
objectives: Use complex formulas and create data visualizations
lessons.experiment: Budget Visualizer: Input financial data using formulas and select the best chart type to represent the trends.
